methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copyTo
methods.description: **RETIRED** Copies this material to the specified target.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the material from the Design. This method only applies to materials in a Design that are unused

properties.name: appearance
properties.description: Gets the Appearance of this material.

properties.name: description
properties.description: Gets and sets the description associated with this material. Setting the description is only valid for materials in a document or the favorites list.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Returns the unique internal ID of this material.

properties.name: isUsed
properties.description: Returns true if this material is used in the Design

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: materialProperties
properties.description: Returns the collection of material properties associated with this material.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Returns the name of this Material. This is the name of the material as seen in the user interface. The name can only be edited if the material is in a Design or the favorites list.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parent
properties.description: Returns the Parent object (a Library or a Design).

appearance.title: Material.appearance Property
appearance.description: Gets the Appearance of this material.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = material_var.appearance

description.title: Material.description Property
description.description: Gets and sets the description associated with this material. Setting the description is only valid for materials in a document or the favorites list.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = material_var.description

# Set the value of the property.
material_var.description = propertyValue

objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

parent.title: Material.parent Property
parent.description: Returns the Parent object (a Library or a Design).

classType.title: Material.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copyTo.title: Material.copyTo Method
copyTo.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Copies this material to the specified target. Remarks
copyTo.return_description: Returns the new copy of the material or null if the copy failed.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target can be a Design or MaterialFavorites object.

deleteMe.title: Material.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes the material from the Design. This method only applies to materials in a Design that are unused
deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the delete was successful.
